new_classes.label_de: alkoholhaltiges Getränk der Position 2208 (Branntwein, Likör u. a.)
new_classes.definition_en: An undenatured alcoholic liquid of less than 80 % vol alcoholic strength, generally intended for human consumption, prepared either directly by distillation (with or without added flavouring) or by other processes such as maceration, infusion, or blending; includes ethyl alcohol, spirits, liqueurs and other spirituous beverages of CN heading 2208.

A process in which an alcoholic liquid or fermented substrate is heated so that alcohol vapour is separated and condensed, yielding a distillate with elevated alcoholic strength; the principal production method for spirits of CN heading 2208.
A quality borne by an alcoholic liquid that represents its concentration of ethyl alcohol expressed as a percentage of volume at 20 °C.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three main distinguishing criteria for heading 2208: (1) undenatured ethyl alcohol (isDenatured = false), (2) alcoholic strength by volume strictly less than 80 % vol (decimalRange maxExclusive 80.0), and (3) production by distillation (someValuesFrom DistillationProcess). A new AlcoholicStrengthByVolumeQuality class is introduced to anchor the strength criterion in BFO via a quality, and the existing eucn:alcoholByVolumePercent datatype property is reused for the numeric range. Coverage is reduced from 1.0 because: (a) the legal text also mentions preparation methods other than distillation (maceration, infusion, blending, compounding) which are not modelled as separate process classes at this heading level; (b) the "generally intended for human consumption" criterion is only partially captured by the isDenatured flag rather than an explicit consumption-intent quality; (c) the note is truncated ("entweder unmittelbar durch Des…") and may contain further distinguishing criteria not visible in the supplied text.
